Home_Badge: Free finance tools · No login · India-first
Home_Sub: Clean, mobile-first calculators and planners. Every tool shows the formula, a worked example, and secure vs volatile ways to reach the number — so you learn the “why”, not just the result.
Home_GainWhyText: Each tool starts with a short explanation so beginners can follow.
Home_GainFormulaText: We show the standard formula used in finance, in simple words.
Home_GainExampleText: Try the default numbers and understand how the result is formed.
Tools_Desc: Clean, structured calculators and planners. Each tool includes examples, assumptions, and a clear “why”.
NotFound_Message: Sorry, there's nothing at this address.
Tool_goal_Description: Secure vs volatile monthly saving to reach any target amount.
Tool_fire_Description: Find your FIRE corpus and SIP needed (inflation-aware).
Tool_fd_Description: Stagger deposits for yearly liquidity and re-investment flexibility.
Tool_sip_Description: Estimate long-term SIP growth with scenarios and projections.
Tool_cagr_Description: Understand annualized growth for any investment.
Tool_lumpsum_Description: Project a one-time investment forward in time.
Tool_emi_Description: Monthly EMI, total interest, and yearly amortization.
Tool_mortgage_Description: Home loan estimate with EMI and affordability.
Tool_inflation_Description: Future cost of today’s money with year-by-year growth.
Tool_interest_Description: Understand compounding on savings and loans.
Page_About_DisclaimerBody: FinYatra provides educational estimates only. We are not a SEBI-registered advisor, bank, or broker. Results depend on the assumptions you enter. Always verify important financial decisions with a qualified professional.
Page_About_Description: FinYatra helps you plan money with clarity — calculators, formulas, and worked examples in plain language.
Page_About_Offer4: Educational content: formulas, assumptions, and step-by-step explanations
Page_Contact_Description: Report a bug, request a feature, ask a question, or discuss a partnership.
Page_ComingSoon_LayoutHint: Use Tiles/List to switch the layout.
Page_Search_NoResultsBody: We couldn't find a tool matching "{0}". Try a shorter keyword like goal, sip, emi, or inflation.
Page_Privacy_Description: FinYatra is a calculator-first, privacy-friendly app. This policy explains what we collect, what we do not, and the choices you have.
Page_ComingSoon_RequestBody: Email your request (and what you want this tool to answer) to inbox.nuthan@gmail.com.
Page_Contact_ConsentText: I agree to the Privacy Policy and Terms.
Page_Contact_PrivacyText: We only use your message to respond. See our Privacy Policy.
Page_About_Offer2: No login, no sign-up, no paywall — use every tool for free
Page_About_ContactBody: Questions, feedback, or partnership enquiries? Visit our Contact page or email inbox.nuthan@gmail.com.
Page_About_Offer1: Goal planning, SIP, EMI, inflation, FIRE, and FD laddering calculators
Info_FormulaIntro: Read the formula first, then follow the steps below to see how the number is built.
Page_Contact_InfoBody: Have feedback on a calculator, spotted a bug, or want a new tool? Send a message using the secure form — we will email you back at the address you provide.
Page_Contact_Subtitle: We'd love to hear from you
Page_Sitemap_Description: A complete list of public pages for visitors and search engines.
Page_About_MaintainerBody: FinYatra is built and maintained by Nuthan Murarysetty — an independent developer passionate about clear, trustworthy finance tools for Indian users.
Page_Search_Description: Search tools by name or description.
Page_Sitemap_Intro: Machine-readable sitemap: sitemap.xml · Also useful for crawlers and AdSense review.
Page_About_MissionBody: FinYatra is a free collection of financial calculators and planners built for India. We believe everyone should understand the why behind the numbers — not just the result. Every tool shows the formula, a worked example, and practical context.
Page_ComingSoon_Description: This tool is planned next. Explore available calculators in the meantime.
Page_Terms_Description: Please read these terms before using FinYatra. By using the app you agree to them.
Page_ComingSoon_NextBody: We are building this calculator with the same formula-first, example-driven approach as the rest of FinYatra.
Page_Contact_SuccessBody: Thank you — your message was delivered securely. We will reply within a few business days.
Page_About_Offer3: Mobile-first design with Hindi, Telugu, Tamil, Kannada, and Malayalam support
Page_Search_SubtitleEmpty: Type in the top search box and press Enter.
Tool_emi_LearnText: How interest rate and tenure change EMI and total interest paid.
Tool_inflation_LearnText: How inflation erodes purchasing power and why planning must account for it.
Tool_sip_LearnText: How time (tenure) and return rate have the biggest impact on long-term wealth.
Tool_goal_LearnText: How much to save monthly in a secure path (FD-like) vs a volatile path (equity-like), and how inflation changes the target.
Tool_fd_LearnText: Split a lump sum into multiple fixed deposits maturing in different years for liquidity.
Tool_fire_LearnText: FIRE helps you plan a corpus so that your investments can cover your yearly expenses.
Tool_goal_StepInflation_5: Multiply by targetToday to get the inflation-adjusted future target.
Tool_goal_FormulaSipNote: If return is 0%, sip = target / months.
Tool_goal_ExampleStep_3: Secure path at 7% FD-like return needs roughly ₹18,500/month.
Tool_goal_StepInflation_2: Convert inflation % to a decimal rate (e.g. 6% → 0.06).
Tool_goal_FormulaInflationNote: If inflation is off, futureTarget = targetToday.
Tool_fd_ExampleStep_2: Create FDs at 1 year, 2 years, 3 years so one FD matures every year.
Tool_goal_StepSip_5: This sip is the monthly amount that compounds to your target.
Tool_fire_ExampleStep_3: Yearly expense at retirement: ~₹19.2 lakh → FIRE corpus target ≈ ₹4.8 crore (25× rule).
Tool_goal_ExampleTitle: Product example: ₹10L car + emergency fund in 5 years
Tool_goal_ScenarioSubtitle: How much you need to invest each month to reach the target
Tool_sip_ExampleStep_5: Key insight: starting early and staying consistent matters more than chasing the highest return.
Tool_goal_Info_Goal_3: Understand the impact of inflation on future required money
Tool_fire_ScenarioSubtitle: Monthly SIP needed to reach FIRE corpus
Tool_goal_Info_RealLife: Buying a car, building an emergency fund, down payment planning, education fund — anything with a target amount and deadline.
Tool_goal_StepSip_4: Apply SIP formula: sip = target × r / ((1 + r) ^ months − 1).
Tool_fire_ExampleStep_5: At 7% secure return, you'd need about ₹92,000/month — volatile path needs less saving but carries risk.
Tool_inflation_ExampleStep_3: In 20 years, that rises to roughly ₹1.6 lakh/month.
Tool_emi_ExampleStep_3: Total payment over 20 years: roughly ₹52 lakh — of which ~₹27 lakh is interest.
Tool_fire_ExampleStep_4: At 10% return, you need roughly ₹66,000/month SIP to reach this.
Tool_goal_Info_Alt_RdFd_Cons: May not beat inflation after tax
Tool_goal_Info_WhyKnow: Most people save without a clear target. This makes your goal measurable and shows how returns and inflation change the required monthly saving.
Tool_inflation_ExampleStep_4: If your income or investments don't grow at least at inflation rate, you're effectively falling behind.
Tool_sip_ExampleStep_4: At 15% (aggressive), corpus can reach about ₹67 lakh.
Tool_goal_Info_Purpose: Convert a goal amount and time horizon into a simple monthly saving plan.
Tool_goal_ExampleStep_4: Volatile base at 12% equity-like return needs roughly ₹16,400/month — but outcomes can range from ~₹19,800 (8%) to ~₹14,600 (15%).
Tool_sip_FormulaNote: FV is the future value of your investment. ^ means 'to the power of'.
Tool_sip_ExampleStep_2: At 10% (conservative), corpus grows to about ₹41 lakh.
Tool_inflation_ExampleStep_2: At 6% inflation, in 10 years you need about ₹89,500/month for the same lifestyle.
Tool_goal_Info_Alt_Budget_When: When you only need a rough plan
Tool_goal_Info_Alt_RdFd_When: When you want a fixed monthly deposit at fixed rate
Tool_goal_Info_Goal_1: Make your goal time-bound and number-driven
Tool_fd_ExampleStep_4: This gives predictable liquidity without breaking a long-term deposit early.
Tool_fd_FormulaNote: P is the deposit, r the annual rate (decimal), t the years. Simplified annual compounding; ^ means 'to the power of'.
Tool_fire_ExampleTitle: Product example: retire at 50 with ₹50,000/month expenses
Tool_goal_Info_Motto: Small steps, steady journey — save with clarity, not anxiety.
Tool_fire_Quote: Save money, and money will save you.
Tool_emi_BreakdownSubtitle: Principal vs interest paid each year
Tool_emi_ExampleStep_5: Always compare EMI affordability vs total interest cost before choosing tenure.
Tool_inflation_FormulaNote: i is the inflation rate per year (as a decimal, e.g. 6% = 0.06). ^ means 'to the power of'.
Tool_goal_StepInflation_4: Raise it to the number of years: (1 + inflationRate) ^ years.
Tool_goal_BreakdownSubtitle: If you save the base volatile monthly amount
Tool_goal_StepSip_1: Convert annual return to monthly: r = annualReturn / 12.
Tool_emi_ExampleStep_4: If you reduce tenure to 15 years, EMI rises but total interest drops significantly.
Tool_inflation_ExampleStep_5: This is why FIRE and goal planning must account for inflation, not just today's numbers.
Tool_goal_Info_Alt_Sip_When: When you are investing in market-linked funds
Tool_goal_ExampleStep_5: Trade-off: secure gives predictable outcome; volatile may need less monthly saving but with uncertainty.
Tool_sip_ExampleStep_1: You invest ₹10,000 every month for 15 years — total ₹18 lakh invested.
Tool_goal_ExampleStep_2: With 6% inflation, the future target becomes about ₹13.4 lakh.
Tool_emi_FormulaNote: P is the loan principal. ^ means 'to the power of'.
Tool_goal_ExampleStep_1: Goal: ₹10,00,000 in today's money for a used car + emergency buffer in 5 years.
Tool_fd_ExampleStep_3: When the 1-year FD matures, you can use it or roll it into a new long FD.
Tool_fire_ExampleStep_2: Monthly expenses ₹50,000 today inflate to about ₹1.6 lakh/month by retirement (6% inflation).
Tool_fire_ExampleStep_1: Current age 30, target retirement at 50 — 20 years to build corpus.
Tool_goal_Info_Differentiation: Instead of just giving one number, FinYatra shows secure vs volatile scenarios side-by-side, and a year-by-year breakdown so users understand uncertainty and progress.
Tool_fire_Info_Purpose: Estimate the retirement corpus needed and the monthly investing required to reach it.
Tool_fire_Info_WhyKnow: Retirement planning is mostly math + discipline. FIRE makes the goal concrete and shows the role of inflation, withdrawal rate assumptions, and return expectations.
Tool_fire_Info_RealLife: Choosing when you can stop full-time work, planning a career break, or ensuring investments can cover living expenses without salary.
Tool_fire_Info_Goal_2: Translate expenses into a corpus target (rule of thumb)
Tool_fire_Info_Goal_3: Compute monthly SIP needed for the time horizon
Tool_fire_Info_Alt_Retirement_When: When you want a more detailed model
Tool_fire_Info_Alt_Rule_When: When you want quick corpus thumb rule
Tool_fire_Info_Alt_Goal_When: When you want to plan any goal, not only retirement
Tool_fire_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra focuses on clarity: fewer inputs, inflation-aware numbers, and a projection table so users understand what the corpus means and how SIP builds it.
Tool_fire_Info_Motto: Freedom is built, not wished — plan it and invest steadily.
Tool_fire_StepInflate_2: Inflate it by assumed inflation for the years until retirement.
Tool_fire_StepInflate_3: This approximates what the same lifestyle costs at retirement.
Tool_fire_StepCorpus_1: Convert future monthly expense to yearly expense.
Tool_fire_FormulaSipTitle: Monthly SIP needed to reach corpus
Tool_fire_StepSipCalc_1: Convert annual return to monthly return r.
Tool_fire_StepSipCalc_3: Solve SIP such that contributions compound to the corpus target.
Tool_sip_Info_Purpose: Estimate how a fixed monthly investment can grow over time with compounding.
Tool_sip_Info_WhyKnow: SIP planning helps you avoid guesswork. It connects your monthly saving habit with a future goal, and shows how time and return assumptions change the outcome.
Tool_sip_Info_RealLife: Building wealth for a house down payment, child education, retirement, or any long-term goal where you invest monthly.
Tool_sip_Info_Goal_1: Estimate future value for a monthly SIP
Tool_sip_Info_Goal_3: See year-by-year growth to build confidence and consistency
Tool_sip_Info_Alt_Lumpsum_When: When you invest once, not monthly
Tool_sip_Info_Alt_Lumpsum_Pros: Simple if you have a lump sum
Tool_sip_Info_Alt_Goal_When: When you start from a target amount
Tool_sip_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra adds structured scenarios and a readable projection table so users understand both the estimate and the uncertainty behind it.
Tool_sip_Info_Motto: Consistency builds wealth — one SIP at a time.
Tool_sip_StepFv_1: Convert annual return to monthly return r = annualReturn/12.
Tool_sip_StepFv_3: ((1+r)^n - 1)/r is the compounding multiplier for repeated monthly contributions.
Tool_sip_StepFv_4: Multiply by SIP amount to get estimated future value.
Tool_sip_StepFv_5: If r is 0%, FV = SIP × n.
Tool_emi_Info_Purpose: Find the fixed monthly payment for a loan, and understand how much goes to interest vs principal over time.
Tool_emi_Info_WhyKnow: EMI affects monthly cashflow. Small changes in rate or tenure can change total interest by lakhs — knowing EMI helps you choose smarter tenures and prepayment strategy.
Tool_emi_Info_RealLife: Home loans, car loans, education loans, personal loans — anywhere you repay principal + interest monthly.
Tool_emi_Info_Goal_1: Know the monthly EMI before taking a loan
Tool_emi_Info_Goal_2: See total interest cost (hidden cost of long tenure)
Tool_emi_Info_Goal_3: Understand amortization (early EMIs pay more interest)
Tool_emi_Info_Alt_Bank_When: When you want a bank-specific offer
Tool_emi_Info_Alt_Amort_When: When you want full monthly schedule
Tool_emi_Info_Alt_Afford_When: When you want a quick check
Tool_emi_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra keeps it minimal but complete: summary cards, scenario comparison, and a readable amortization breakdown so users understand the real cost of borrowing.
Tool_emi_Info_Motto: Borrow wisely — keep EMIs calm and predictable.
Tool_emi_StepEmi_1: P is your loan principal (loan amount).
Tool_emi_StepEmi_2: Convert annual interest rate to monthly rate: monthlyRate = annualRate/12.
Tool_emi_StepEmi_3: n is the number of months (years × 12).
Tool_emi_StepEmi_4: The formula gives the fixed payment that exactly pays principal+interest by month n.
Tool_emi_StepEmi_5: If rate is 0%, EMI = P / n.
Tool_inflation_Info_Purpose: Estimate how the cost of something increases over time due to inflation.
Tool_inflation_Info_WhyKnow: Most financial goals fail because people plan in today's rupees. Inflation quietly increases your required amount — especially for long horizons like retirement and education.
Tool_inflation_Info_RealLife: Future school fees, future rent, medical costs, lifestyle expenses in retirement, long-term projects like house construction.
Tool_inflation_Info_Goal_1: Convert today's cost into future cost
Tool_inflation_Info_Goal_2: Understand the compounding nature of inflation
Tool_inflation_Info_Goal_3: Make FIRE and goal planning realistic
Tool_inflation_Info_Alt_Rule72_When: When you want quick doubling time
Tool_inflation_Info_Alt_Cpi_When: When you need official inflation series
Tool_inflation_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra pairs the formula with a year-by-year table and real examples so users feel the impact, not just see a single number.
Tool_inflation_Info_Motto: Plan for tomorrow's rupee, not today's rupee.
Tool_inflation_StepFuture_2: Convert inflation % to a growth factor (1 + inflationRate).
Tool_inflation_StepFuture_3: Raise it to the number of years (compounding).
Tool_fd_Info_Purpose: Plan staggered fixed deposits so you get periodic maturities instead of locking everything into one date.
Tool_fd_Info_WhyKnow: Breaking an FD early often reduces interest. Laddering reduces the need to break deposits while keeping money relatively safe and available on schedule.
Tool_fd_Info_RealLife: Emergency fund parking, planned expenses (fees, travel, home renovation), and conservative investors who want predictable cashflow.
Tool_fd_Info_Goal_3: Balance return vs access to cash
Tool_fd_Info_Alt_Single_When: When you don't need interim liquidity
Tool_fd_Info_Alt_Rd_When: When you invest monthly not lump sum
Tool_fd_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra shows the ladder in a clean table with the per-FD split and maturity estimate so you can plan liquidity year-by-year.
Tool_fd_Info_Motto: Keep money safe — without locking yourself in.
Tool_fd_StepLadder_3: For each FD, estimate maturity using simplified annual compounding.
Tool_fd_StepLadder_4: Result is a ladder where one FD matures each year.
Page_Privacy_S01_Body: FinYatra ("we", "us", "the app") provides free educational financial calculators and planners. We are an independent project maintained by Nuthan Murarysetty. You can reach us anytime at
Page_Privacy_S02_L1: No account, sign-up, or login is required to use FinYatra.
Page_Privacy_S02_L2: The numbers you type into calculators are processed in your browser and are not sent to our servers.
Page_Privacy_S02_L3: We do not sell your personal data — ever.
Page_Privacy_S02_L4: We use privacy-respecting analytics and, where enabled, advertising to keep the app free.
Page_Privacy_S03_Body: Calculator inputs (for example target amounts, interest rates, tenure, and expenses) are used only to compute results on your device. They stay in your browser session and are cleared when you close or refresh the page, unless you deliberately copy or share a result. We do not store these values on a server or link them to your identity.
Page_Privacy_S04_Body: Like most websites, our hosting provider and any analytics or ad partners may automatically receive standard technical data such as your IP address, device and browser type, approximate region, referring page, and pages viewed. This helps us keep the site secure, understand which tools are useful, and fix problems.
Page_Privacy_S05_Body: We use a small amount of local storage to remember display preferences (such as your Tiles/List choice). If advertising is enabled, third parties may set cookies as described below. You can clear cookies and local storage at any time from your browser settings.
Page_Privacy_S06_Body: To keep FinYatra free, we may display ads through Google AdSense. Google and its partners may use cookies and device identifiers to serve and measure ads, including personalized ads based on your prior visits, subject to your settings. You can control ad personalization at
Page_Privacy_S06_LinkPolicy: How Google uses information from sites that use its services
Page_Privacy_S07_Body: We load the Inter font from Google Fonts and some libraries from public CDNs to render the app. These providers may receive your IP address as part of delivering their files. External links to sources (RBI, SEBI, banks, and similar) are provided for reference; their privacy practices are governed by their own policies.
Page_Privacy_S08_L1: Use the app without providing any personal information.
Page_Privacy_S08_L2: Block or delete cookies and local storage in your browser.
Page_Privacy_S08_L3: Opt out of personalized ads via your Google account or browser controls.
Page_Privacy_S08_L4: Contact us to ask any privacy question or raise a concern.
Page_Privacy_S09_Body: FinYatra is intended for a general audience and is not directed at children under 13. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children.
Page_Privacy_S10_Body: We may update this policy as the app evolves. Material changes will be reflected here with a new "last updated" date. Continued use of FinYatra after changes means you accept the revised policy.
Page_Terms_S01_Body: By accessing or using FinYatra, you agree to be bound by these Terms and Conditions and our
Page_Terms_S01_BodyAfter: If you do not agree, please do not use the app.
Page_Terms_S02_Title: 2. Educational use only — not financial advice
Page_Terms_S02_Body: FinYatra provides general educational calculators, estimates, and explanations. It is not financial, investment, tax, or legal advice, and it does not create an advisor–client relationship. Results are illustrative and depend on the assumptions you enter. Always consult a qualified professional and verify figures with official sources before making financial decisions.
Page_Terms_S03_Body: We aim to keep formulas, examples, and rate assumptions correct and up to date, but we make no guarantee of accuracy, completeness, or timeliness. Interest rates, returns, inflation, and tax rules change frequently and vary by provider. You are responsible for confirming critical numbers independently.
Page_Terms_S04_Body: Projections use simplified models (for example fixed annual compounding). Real investments involve risk, fees, taxes, and market fluctuations, and may perform very differently from any estimate shown here. Past performance and sample returns do not guarantee future results.
Page_Terms_S05_L1: Do not misuse the app, attempt to disrupt it, or access it through automated abuse.
Page_Terms_S05_L2: Do not present FinYatra results as professional advice to others.
Page_Terms_S05_L3: Do not copy the app to impersonate it or mislead users.
Page_Terms_S06_Body: The FinYatra name, design, layout, and original content are owned by their author. You may use the calculators for personal, non-commercial purposes and share screenshots of your own results. All other rights are reserved.
Page_Terms_S07_Body: FinYatra may link to third-party sites (such as RBI, SEBI, and banks) and may display advertising. We do not control and are not responsible for third-party content, products, or practices. Visiting third-party sites is at your own risk and subject to their terms.
Page_Terms_S08_Body: To the maximum extent permitted by law, FinYatra and its author are not liable for any direct, indirect, incidental, or consequential losses arising from your use of, or reliance on, the app or its results. The app is provided "as is" and "as available" without warranties of any kind.
Page_Terms_S09_Body: We may modify, suspend, or discontinue any part of the app at any time, and we may update these terms. Continued use after changes means you accept the updated terms.
Page_Terms_S10_Body: Interface icons are simple line icons rendered as inline SVG, based on the open-source
Page_Terms_S10_BodyMid: icon style (ISC / MIT licensed). The typeface is
Page_Terms_S10_BodyAfter: by Rasmus Andersson under the SIL Open Font License. If you believe any asset needs additional attribution, contact
Page_Terms_S10_BodyEnd: and we will address it promptly.
